name: Impossible Travel Detected
notes: This rule triggers when successful authentication is detected from locations that are impossible to travel to within a short period of time based on speed of travel and distance.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
when the event matches Username is not N/A
when the event context is Remote to Local
when the event matches GEO::LOOKUP(sourceip, 'city_name') != '{"city":{"name":null}}' AND GEO::LOOKUP(sourceip, 'city_name') != '{}' AQL filter query

when the event matches (GEO::DISTANCE(sourceip, REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','ipKey',username))/DOUBLE(DOUBLE(starttime-REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','dateKey',username))/3600000)) > 1600.0 and GEO::DISTANCE(sourceip, REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','ipKey',username)) > 800 AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Impossible Travel Detected on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
AND when the event matches Username is not N/A
AND when the event context is Remote to Local
AND when the event matches GEO::LOOKUP(sourceip, 'city_name') != '{"city":{"name":null}}' AND GEO::LOOKUP(sourceip, 'city_name') != '{}' AQL filter query

AND when the event matches (GEO::DISTANCE(sourceip, REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','ipKey',username))/DOUBLE(DOUBLE(starttime-REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','dateKey',username))/3600000)) > 1600.0 and GEO::DISTANCE(sourceip, REFERENCETABLE('Impossible Travel','ipKey',username)) > 800 AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects when successful authentication is detected from locations that are impossible to travel to within a short period of time based on speed of travel and distance.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
Add the "sourceIP" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Impossible Travel Users"
